capabilities.normalized_requirement: Provide one governed inline Jarvis assistant with selectable models, evidence, protected fields and owner-gated actions.
capabilities.remaining_gap: Authenticated deployed popup interaction, tooltip/action UX and live-provider evidence.

capabilities.normalized_requirement: Integrate n8n through a default-off, signed, idempotent, replay-resistant and auditable adapter.
capabilities.remaining_gap: Authorised n8n staging endpoint, vault reference, workflow inventory, canary and rollback proof.

capabilities.normalized_requirement: Manage scoped instructions and editable Jarvis objects with precedence, protected fields, diffs, approvals, tests and rollback.
capabilities.remaining_gap: Connected multi-repository editing and authenticated staging.

capabilities.normalized_requirement: Operate deployment, health, incident, backup, restore and rollback through independent release controls.
capabilities.remaining_gap: Live topology, Recovery Console repair, staging health, restore and rollback evidence.

capabilities.normalized_requirement: Provide provenance-aware hybrid research, retrieval, vector and knowledge-graph services without treating retrieved text as executable truth.
capabilities.remaining_gap: Connected PostgreSQL/pgvector staging, permissions benchmark and current external-source adapters.

capabilities.normalized_requirement: Provide tenant-safe read-only database inspection with query allowlists, limits, timeouts, audit and separately approved mutations.
capabilities.remaining_gap: Design and implement the generic inspector against a bounded local fixture before any live database connection.

decision_rules: Source prose is not implementation evidence.
Duplicate capabilities inherit the strongest current Jarvis controls rather than creating weaker parallel modules.
Live integration remains separate from repository-local deterministic implementation.
The database inspector remains BACKLOGGED until code and tests exist.
completion_assertion: All six retrieved capability candidates are dispositioned and traceable; this does not establish whole-file source coverage or whole-program completion.
